speaker: Alice
content: Susan awoke that night alone and parched, but as she gazed out of her window, she saw the tall drink of water she needed to quench her thirst.
mark: wake vi/vt. 唤醒，醒来；唤起，激起；n. 守夜，守灵 → woke → woken
awake vi/vt. （使）醒来，唤醒；唤起adj. 醒着的 → awoke → awoken
awaken vi/vt. 唤醒；唤起； 觉醒；醒来 → awakened → awakened
三个词在这里可以互换，他们从上到下一个比一个正式。
parch v. 烤，烘 parched adj. 焦的；炎热的；炒过的；干透的；非常口渴的
gaze v. 凝视，注视
the tall drink of water 俚语，形容高大英俊的男人
quench v. 止渴；满足
quench one's thirst 解渴

speaker: Julie
content: "Dear diary, Mike doesn't even know I'm alive."
mark: Dear diary 日记常用开头
even 对句子中的某个成分进行强调时，它通常放在实义动词之前、助动词或情态动词之后

speaker: Julie
content: If you want to date him, you're going to have to ask him out.
mark: date sb 和某人约会
ask sb out 约某人出去
be going to have to do sth 将要不得不做某事

speaker: Susan
content: I keep hoping he'll ask me out.
mark: keep hoping 一直希望 I keep hoping that ... 我一直希望……；持续期望着……

speaker: Julie
mark: How's that going? 进展如何？那进行得怎么样了？

speaker: Susan
content: Shouldn't you be making brownies for your nerdy friends?
mark: Shouldn't you be doing sth? 你难道不该在做某事吗？
brownie n. 布朗尼蛋糕
nerdy adj. 书呆子气的

speaker: Julie
content: I can't find the measuring cup. Have you seen it?

speaker: Susan
content: The measuring cup? Well, it's got to be here somewhere. Just keep looking.
mark: it's got to be here somewhere 它一定在这里的某个地方 it's got to be = it has got to be 一定是
somewhere 某个地方
